records.name: Kansas City Free Eye Clinic
records.what: Charitable nonprofit (founded 2008) providing free comprehensive eye exams and free prescription eyeglasses to the underserved KC metro population. Walk-in exams Wednesdays 9am-12pm; other services Fridays 9am-12pm. Glasses made locally and provided at no charge via Sabates Eye Centers. Located at the Hope Faith homeless campus. 100% free for homeless / short-term housing individuals (support letter needed); a $30-45 contribution is requested from others. Must be a KS or MO resident; lifetime cap of 4 exams / 4 pairs of glasses.
records.free_claim: Site states it provides free eye care and corrective lenses to the underserved; 100% free care for homeless or short-term housing individuals (support letter), with glasses provided at no charge by Sabates Eye Centers. (A $30-45 contribution is requested from non-homeless patients.)
records.nonprofit_claim: Registered 501(c)(3) charitable nonprofit (GuideStar EIN 27-0704299), operating from the Hope Faith Homeless Assistance Campus.

records.name: Catholic Charities of Kansas City-St. Joseph — Free Eye Clinic
records.what: Catholic Charities runs free, no-cost vision screening clinics where families in need have their eyes examined by a doctor and, when eligible, receive a free pair of prescription eyeglasses (clinic also includes hearing and blood-sugar screening). No insurance required. Recurring clinics at rotating sites; the KC metro clinic is at the Serve&Lift center, 8001 Longview Rd, Kansas City, MO 64134 (walk-ins accepted there, no appointment needed).
records.free_claim: Site states the clinics provide 'no-cost vision screenings and, when eligible, free eyeglasses,' and that 'no insurance is required.'
records.nonprofit_claim: Faith-based charity (Catholic Charities of Kansas City-St. Joseph), a registered nonprofit social-services organization.

records.name: KU Eye Lions Vision Clinics (University of Kansas Dept. of Ophthalmology)
records.what: Quarterly free vision clinics run by the KU Eye Lions Club (KU Eye physicians, residents and staff) for people who are uninsured and cannot otherwise afford eye care in the KC metro. Free eye exams; free or discounted surgeries on a case-by-case basis. Access is by referral form (emailed to dkephart@kumc.edu); patients are called to confirm an appointment about two weeks before the clinic date.
records.free_claim: Site states the KU Eye Lions Club holds 'quarterly free vision clinics for those who are uninsured and cannot otherwise afford eyecare,' plus free or discounted surgeries case-by-case. (Site does not explicitly state eyeglasses are free.)
records.nonprofit_claim: Volunteer charitable clinic operated by the KU Eye Lions Club (a Lions service-club affiliate) within an academic medical center's outreach program.

records.name: New Eyes
records.what: National by-application charity that provides a free new basic pair of prescription eyeglasses (single-vision or lined bifocal) via an e-voucher to low-income individuals across the U.S. (serves KS and MO). Requires a current eyeglass prescription (within 24 months, with pupillary distance) and household net income below 250% of the federal poverty guidelines. Social-service agencies can apply on a client's behalf, or individuals may apply directly with proof of eligibility. No fee to approved applicants for the glasses (New Eyes does not cover the cost of the exam itself).
records.free_claim: Site states New Eyes provides new prescription eyeglasses at no cost to eligible low-income applicants through its e-voucher program; the voucher covers a basic pair with no fee to the applicant.
records.nonprofit_claim: New Eyes (New Eyes for the Needy) is a national registered nonprofit charity buying eyeglasses for low-income individuals in the U.S.

records.name: EyeCare America (American Academy of Ophthalmology)
records.what: National by-application public-service program connecting eligible uninsured/underinsured people with volunteer ophthalmologists who provide a medical eye exam at no out-of-pocket cost (plus up to one year of follow-up for conditions diagnosed at the exam). Seniors Program: U.S. citizens/legal residents age 65+, not in an HMO, no VA eye benefits, and not seen by an ophthalmologist in 3+ years. Serves KS and MO via local volunteer ophthalmologists. Note: covers medical eye exams only — does NOT provide eyeglasses or eyeglass prescriptions.
records.free_claim: Site states EyeCare America provides eligible patients a medical eye exam through volunteer ophthalmologists at no out-of-pocket cost, with up to one year of follow-up care for diagnosed conditions. (Does not cover eyeglasses.)
records.nonprofit_claim: Public-service program of the Foundation of the American Academy of Ophthalmology, delivered by volunteer ophthalmologists nationwide.

records.name: Lions Club Local Eyeglass / Eye Exam Sponsorship (KC metro local clubs incl. Overland Park Host Lions, Olathe Lions)
records.what: Lions Clubs International service clubs sponsor eligible low-income individuals for a free eye exam and free eyeglasses through their local club. Applicants generally qualify if total monthly income is at or below 200% of the federal poverty level and they have no vision coverage. Apply by contacting your local Lions Club (Lions 'Find a Club' locator / 'Get Help' form); KC-metro area clubs include the Overland Park Host Lions Club and the Olathe Lions Club. Volunteer-run, so processing can take weeks to months and assistance depends on local club funds.
records.free_claim: Lions guidance states that if your monthly income is at or below the 200% poverty level and you have no vision coverage, you may qualify to be sponsored by your local Lions Club for a (free) eye exam and eyeglasses.
records.nonprofit_claim: Lions Clubs International is a volunteer service-club humanitarian organization; vision/eyeglass assistance is a long-standing charitable program of local Lions Clubs.

records.name: WashU Medicine Free Eye Service (WUFES)
records.what: Student-run mobile eye service offering free vision screenings and reading glasses for uninsured/underinsured patients, with referrals for comprehensive care. Held on select Fridays at rotating partner sites: Casa de Salud, WashU Medicine Pro Bono Health Clinic, and St. Patrick's Center in St. Louis. Walk-in, no appointment needed.
records.free_claim: Org states it offers 'free eye health education and vision screening services for uninsured and underinsured patients'; completely free/charitable, not sliding scale.
records.nonprofit_claim: Charitable service run by WashU Medicine physicians and medical students for underserved populations.

records.name: New Eyes
records.what: National by-application program providing free new prescription eyeglasses to low-income children and adults via an e-voucher redeemed on its ordering site. Serves Eastern Missouri residents. Requires a household income at or below 250% of poverty guidelines and an eye exam within the last 24 months (does not pay for exams). Best applied for through a social-service agency.
records.free_claim: 501(c)(3) provides free new prescription eyeglasses; approved applicants receive an e-voucher redeemable for a free pair of eyeglasses (no cost to recipient).
records.nonprofit_claim: 501(c)(3) not-for-profit, non-sectarian charitable organization.

records.name: Lions Clubs of District 26-M2 - Eye Care Assistance
records.what: St. Louis-area Lions Clubs (District 26-M2) provide free eyeglasses and eye-care assistance to people in need through an application/request-for-assistance form, in partnership with the nonprofit Saving Sight (Missouri Lions Eye Research Foundation). Separate application available for eye surgery assistance. Administered by local clubs across the St. Louis metro.
records.free_claim: Provides charitable assistance for eyeglasses and eye care to those in need via application; described as charitable vision services (not sliding scale or insurance).
records.nonprofit_claim: Service-club (Lions Club) charitable program; partners with nonprofit Saving Sight.

records.name: New Eyes (New Eyes for the Needy)
records.free_claim: National nonprofit that provides NEW prescription eyeglasses at no cost to people in financial need, via a voucher redeemed at partner retailers. Applicant must have an eyeglass prescription dated within the last 24 months (incl. PD). The Missouri exclusion on their site applies only to their online vision test, not to the free-glasses voucher program, so Missouri residents can apply for the glasses with an existing prescription.
records.nonprofit_claim: 501(c)(3) charitable nonprofit (New Eyes for the Needy, Short Hills, NJ); free glasses funded by donations.
records.what: By-application free prescription eyeglasses for low-income adults and children (household net monthly income below 250% of federal poverty guidelines). Best applied for through a social-service agency/health advocate on the client's behalf.

records.name: EyeCare America - Seniors Program & Glaucoma Program
records.free_claim: Public-service program of the American Academy of Ophthalmology. Matches eligible patients with a local VOLUNTEER ophthalmologist. Seniors Program: medical eye exam at no out-of-pocket cost for citizens/legal residents 65+ who haven't seen an ophthalmologist in 3+ years and are not in an HMO/VA; uninsured patients receive care free. Glaucoma Program: free baseline glaucoma exam for eligible uninsured at-risk adults. NOTE: does not cover eyeglasses or prescriptions.
records.nonprofit_claim: Charitable program of the Foundation of the American Academy of Ophthalmology, delivered by a national network of volunteer ophthalmologists; serves Missouri including the Columbia/Jefferson City/Hannibal/Kirksville region.
records.what: By-application free medical eye exams (not glasses) for qualifying uninsured/underinsured seniors and at-risk-for-glaucoma adults; care delivered by a volunteer ophthalmologist near the patient.

records.name: Mid-South Lions Sight and Hearing Service
records.free_claim: Provides sight-saving surgeries and eye care at NO COST to indigent patients. A patient must be sponsored by a local Lions Club, which submits an application; federal financial (indigency) guidelines apply and all sight applications require a referral letter from an eyecare professional. Surgeries are provided free; local Lions Clubs provide the eyeglasses.
records.nonprofit_claim: 501(c)(3) nonprofit (Mid-South Lions Sight and Hearing Service, Memphis TN) serving TN, AR, MS, and MISSOURI; partnered with Missouri Lions Clubs and volunteer medical professionals.
records.what: By-application (via a local Missouri Lions Club) free sight-saving eye surgery / eye care for indigent residents who cannot afford it; not a walk-in site.

records.name: Missouri Lions Clubs - Local Eyeglass Assistance
records.free_claim: Local Missouri Lions Clubs sponsor eye exams and eyeglasses for low-income residents with no/limited vision coverage (typically at or below 200% of poverty). Applicant completes an application to the local club, provides verification of need, and the club covers exam/glasses subject to available club funds. Free to the qualifying recipient; recycled/donated glasses are also distributed to those in need.
records.nonprofit_claim: Volunteer service-club charity (Lions Clubs International / Missouri Lions); clubs are nonprofit and volunteer-run.
records.what: By-application free eye exams and eyeglasses through one's nearest Missouri Lions Club. Apply via a local club (e.g. Columbia, Jefferson City, Hannibal, Kirksville area clubs); processing can take several weeks/months.

records.name: Jefferson City Host Lions Club
records.free_claim: Local Lions Club providing vision-care assistance as part of its 'We Serve' mission; collects/recycles donated eyeglasses and can sponsor low-income residents for free eye exams/eyeglasses through Lions eyeglass-assistance and Mid-South Lions referral channels. Assistance is requested directly through the club and is subject to club funds.
records.nonprofit_claim: Volunteer service-club charity (Lions Clubs International), chartered 1925; nonprofit, volunteer-run.
records.what: Local Lions Club serving Jefferson City/Cole County; point of contact to apply for free Lions eyeglass/eye-exam assistance. Meets Wednesdays 12:05-12:55pm.
records.address: Hy-Vee Community Room (upstairs), 3721 W Truman Blvd

records.name: Columbia 20/20 Lions Club
records.free_claim: Local Lions Club active in vision service - collects and sorts donated eyeglasses (monthly at MLERF) for free redistribution to people in need, and can serve as the sponsoring club for free Lions eyeglass-assistance and Mid-South Lions sight programs for low-income Boone County residents. Assistance requested through the club, subject to club funds.
records.nonprofit_claim: Volunteer service-club charity (Lions Clubs International, Columbia MO chapter); nonprofit, volunteer-run.
records.what: Local Lions Club serving Columbia/Boone County; contact point to apply for free Lions eyeglass/eye-exam assistance and recycled donated glasses.

records.name: Springfield Noon Lions Club - Lions4Sight / HELP2020 Vision Assistance
records.free_claim: Lions4Sight provides free eye exams and free single-vision eyeglasses (one pair every two years) to adults at or below the federal poverty level; HELP2020 provides free eye exams and eyeglasses to adults facing financial hardship. Funded by Lions fundraisers and private donations; no fee charged to qualifying applicants.
records.nonprofit_claim: Lions Club (service club / charitable). Springfield Noon Lions Club Foundation is a registered nonprofit (GuideStar/Candid profile EIN 37-1266968).
records.what: Free eye exams and free single-vision eyeglasses for low-income / financial-hardship adults, by application. Submit application and a letter describing your situation by email for review.

records.name: Branson-Hollister Lions Club - Eyeglass Recycling & KidSight Vision Screening
records.free_claim: Distributes free recycled eyeglasses to residents in need in Stone and Taney Counties (over $16,760 in glasses distributed 2009-2014) and conducts free children's vision screenings through the Missouri Lions KidSight program at no cost, referring children to optometrists for follow-up.
records.nonprofit_claim: Lions Club (volunteer service club / charitable). Funds local vision projects through club fundraising (distributes over $35,000/year to Lions projects).
records.what: Free recycled eyeglasses for the needy and free children's vision screenings (KidSight) in Stone/Taney County. Contact the club to request assistance.

records.name: New Eyes (New Eyes for the Needy)
records.free_claim: Provides free new prescription eyeglasses via e-voucher to low-income individuals (household net monthly income below 250% of US Poverty Guidelines) who have a current eyeglass prescription (within 24 months, including PD). Glasses are free to the recipient; a small non-refundable application processing fee may apply.
records.nonprofit_claim: 501(c)(3) nonprofit, non-sectarian charitable organization.
records.what: By-application national program serving Missouri residents. Apply online (ideally through a social-service agency/health advocate) with proof of eligibility and a valid prescription to receive a voucher for free glasses.

records.name: EyeCare America - Seniors Program (Foundation of the American Academy of Ophthalmology)
records.free_claim: Connects eligible seniors 65+ (US citizens/legal residents, no HMO or VA eye benefits, not seen an ophthalmologist in 3+ years) with local volunteer ophthalmologists for a medical eye exam at no out-of-pocket cost, plus up to one year of follow-up care for any condition diagnosed. If uninsured, the care is free. Does NOT cover eyeglasses or eyeglass prescriptions.
records.nonprofit_claim: Public-service program of the Foundation of the American Academy of Ophthalmology (nonprofit); volunteer ophthalmologists.
records.what: Free medical eye exam (not glasses) for qualifying low-income/uninsured seniors 65+ via a referral to a local volunteer ophthalmologist. Apply via online questionnaire.

records.name: Missouri Lions Clubs - Recycled Eyeglasses / Local Club Vision Assistance
records.free_claim: Missouri Lions collect, clean, and redistribute recycled prescription eyeglasses free to people in need, and local Missouri Lions clubs sponsor free eye exams and glasses for qualifying low-income/uninsured individuals. Assistance is requested through a local Lions club.
records.nonprofit_claim: Lions Clubs International / Missouri Lions (service club / charitable network).
records.what: Free recycled eyeglasses and local-club-sponsored free vision assistance. Residents of SW/South-Central Missouri should contact their nearest Lions club to request sponsorship for an exam and glasses.

records.name: Catholic Charities of Kansas City-St. Joseph - Free Eye Exam & Eyeglasses Clinic (St. Joseph)
records.what: Recurring free clinic offering free comprehensive eye exams and free prescription eyeglasses (plus hearing/color/blood-sugar screening), run in partnership with the Lions Club. Held at the St. Joseph office and other NW Missouri towns (e.g., Albany). Appointment via online form; first-come, limited slots.
records.free_claim: Org states 'free eye exams and free eye glasses,' 'no cost to participants,' 'no insurance required.'
records.nonprofit_claim: Catholic Charities (501(c)(3) charity) partnered with the Lions Club; charitable program targeting uninsured/low-income.

records.name: St. Joseph Lions Clubs Eyeglasses Program (via AFL-CIO Community Services)
records.what: By-application program: the three St. Joseph-area Lions Clubs fund eye exams (provided by local optometrists, funded by United Way) and free eyeglasses for uninsured/low-income adults and children in St. Joseph, including those on Medicare/Medicaid between prescription renewals. Apply through the agency; Family Guidance provides exam referrals.
records.free_claim: Glasses distributed to people 'without insurance or medical assistance'; clients receive a free pair (2024: 23 people received glasses valued ~$1,010).
records.nonprofit_claim: Local Lions Clubs (service clubs) + AFL-CIO Community Services + United Way charitable partnership.

records.name: Lawrence Noon Lions Club - Vision Assistance Program
records.what: By-application program partnering with local eye doctors to provide eye exams and eyeglasses for people in need in the Lawrence area. Also runs free early-childhood vision screenings and supports the Kansas Lions Mobile Screening Unit (free vision/diabetes/hearing/BP screenings). Contact the club to request assistance.
records.free_claim: Club 'sponsors a Vision Assistance Program, which partners with local eye doctors to provide exams and glasses for those in need'; screenings described as free.
records.nonprofit_claim: Lions Club (volunteer service club); supports Kansas Lions Sight Foundation.

records.name: Topeka Lions Club / Topeka Lions Foundation - Vision Assistance Program
records.what: By-application vision assistance program funded by the Topeka Lions Foundation, plus free vision screenings for children ages 3-6 at preschools/daycares/schools and used-eyeglass collection. Contact the club to inquire about eyeglass/exam assistance for those in need.
records.free_claim: Provides free children's vision screenings; runs a Vision Assistance Program for financial need (no charge to recipients per club description).
records.nonprofit_claim: Lions Club (volunteer service club) / Topeka Lions Foundation.

records.name: Kansas Lions Sight Foundation
records.what: Statewide Kansas charitable foundation supporting sight services: vision/glaucoma/diabetes screenings, eye-injury and disease treatment assistance, and support for the visually impaired. Eyeglass/exam assistance is requested through your LOCAL Kansas Lions club, which determines aid based on need and club funds.
records.free_claim: Charitable, donor-funded sight assistance; eyeglass/exam help via local Lions clubs is provided at no cost to qualifying low-income, uninsured Kansans.
records.nonprofit_claim: Public, non-profit, tax-exempt Kansas foundation funded by Lions and donors.

records.name: New Eyes (New Eyes for the Needy)
records.what: Nationwide (serves KS and MO) by-application program providing a free basic pair of prescription eyeglasses via an online dispenser. Apply online (ideally through a social-service agency); requires an eyeglasses prescription dated within the last 24 months including PD. Does not pay for exams.
records.free_claim: Approved applicants 'pay no out-of-pocket costs to receive their New Eyes glasses' (small non-refundable application fee for administration may apply).
records.nonprofit_claim: 501(c)(3) non-profit, non-sectarian organization.

records.name: Saving Sight (Missouri Lions Eye Research Foundation) - KidSight & ReSpectacle
records.what: Missouri Lions charitable foundation serving MO, KS, and central IL. Funds KidSight free children's vision screenings and follow-up (including free exams and glasses at mobile clinic days), and partners with ReSpectacle to distribute free recycled eyeglasses via an online database. Children's screenings/glasses arranged through local Lions/schools; recycled glasses ordered online.
records.free_claim: KidSight delivers 'free eye exams' and free pairs of glasses to students in need; ReSpectacle distributes used glasses 'for free.'
records.nonprofit_claim: 501(c)(3) non-profit (Missouri Lions Eye Research Foundation dba Saving Sight).

records.name: Kansas Lions Sight Foundation (apply via local Lions Club)
records.free_claim: If your total monthly income is at or below 200% of the poverty level and you do not have vision coverage, you may qualify to be sponsored by your local Lions Club for a free eye exam and eyeglasses. Apply via your local Lions Club / the Kansas Lions Get Help form.
records.nonprofit_claim: The Kansas Lions Sight Foundation is a public, non-profit, tax-exempt corporation funded by Lions and donor contributions, disbursing funds for sight-related projects across Kansas.
records.what: Statewide by-application assistance: a sponsored free eye exam and a pair of eyeglasses for income-eligible, uninsured Kansas residents, administered through local Lions Clubs (covers central/western Kansas towns including Wichita, Hutchinson, Hays, Dodge City, Garden City).

records.name: Newton Lions Club Eyeglasses Assistance
records.free_claim: Eligible individuals, after Lions Club review of an application, may receive an eye exam and a pair of glasses through this Lions charitable program (Kansas Lions program criteria: at/below 200% poverty and no vision coverage).
records.nonprofit_claim: Newton Lions Club is a volunteer Lions service club operating under the Kansas Lions / Kansas Lions Sight Foundation charitable framework.
records.what: By-application eye exam and eyeglasses assistance for income-eligible residents in the Newton / central Kansas area. Application available at Miller Insurance Agency or downloadable; submit in person (ask for Elaine Brown).
records.address: 616 N. Main St., Newton, KS 67114 (Miller Insurance Agency drop-off)

records.name: New Eyes
records.free_claim: Provides a free voucher for a basic pair of new prescription eyeglasses (clear single-vision or lined bifocal lenses) to applicants in financial need (household net monthly income below 250% of US Poverty Guidelines) with a prescription dated within the last 24 months. A small non-refundable application processing fee applies; the eyeglasses themselves are free via voucher.
records.nonprofit_claim: New Eyes (New Eyes for the Needy) is a charitable nonprofit founded in 1932, based in Short Hills, NJ, that provides free eyeglasses to children and adults nationwide facing financial hardship.
records.what: National by-application free-eyeglasses voucher program; serves residents of central and western Kansas (no walk-in site). Social service agencies can apply on a client's behalf, or individuals may apply directly with proof of eligibility.

records.name: EyeCare America (Seniors Program)
records.free_claim: Connects eligible seniors age 65 and older who lack vision insurance with local volunteer ophthalmologists who provide a medical eye exam at no out-of-pocket cost, plus up to one year of follow-up care for any condition diagnosed during the initial exam.
records.nonprofit_claim: EyeCare America is a public-service program of the American Academy of Ophthalmology Foundation (a nonprofit) delivered by volunteer ophthalmologists.
records.what: National by-application program matching income/age-eligible uninsured seniors (65+) with volunteer ophthalmologists for a no-cost medical eye exam; available to residents of central and western Kansas (no fixed walk-in address). Note: this is an eye exam/medical eye care program, not an eyeglasses program.
